original_hypothesis.title: ProxyOracle can be set to a malicious oracle that reverts or returns extreme rates, bricking borrows/removals or enabling undercollateralized debt
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ProxyOracle fully trusts oracleImplementation. If the owner changes oracleImplementation to a malicious contract, Cauldron updateExchangeRate() and any downstream solvency logic can be forced to revert (DoS) or return manipulated exchange rates that enable unsafe borrowing or forced liquidations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although requires owner action/compromise (LOW realism), the operational blast radius is large because it can affect every consumer of ProxyOracle simultaneously.
deployment_assumptions: ProxyOracle is used as oracle (directly or indirectly) by Cauldron(s) or other risk-critical components
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No explicit wiring shown in deployment scripts for ProxyOracle usage; must confirm in deployed cauldrons/oracle setup.

proof_of_signal.pattern: oracleImplementation = newOracle; // no validation of interface behavior or sanity bounds

preconditions: ProxyOracle owner changes oracleImplementation to an oracle returning unsafe values or reverting
A Cauldron or other component relies on ProxyOracle.get/peek/peekSpot

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Oracle indirection layer with unrestricted implementation swap; no sanity checks

operational_questions: Which production contracts consume ProxyOracle outputs?
Are there monitoring controls or off-chain guards before changing oracleImplementation?
Do consumers clamp rates or handle reverts gracefully?
validation_requirements: Trace oracle addresses used in Cauldron deployments
Simulate oracleImplementation reverting and observe system behavior (borrows, removals, liquidations)
Simulate extreme rates and observe solvency checks and liquidation outcomes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Change oracleImplementation to a contract returning manipulated rate (e.g., near 0 or extremely high)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger Cauldron.updateExchangeRate / actions requiring solvency checks
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Cause DoS (reverts) or enable undercollateralized borrow / forced liquidation cascades

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Indirect protocol control via oracle-driven risk engine manipulation
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Borrow/removal/liquidation flows can be bricked or made unsafe
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High (requires oracle rollback and potential bad-debt handling)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All oracle-dependent lending and liquidation operations

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: High impact during high utilization/volatile collateral periods

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Oracle implementation changes; sudden exchange rate discontinuities
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Spike in insolvencies/liquidations or borrow/withdraw reverts
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Oracle changes followed by large borrow activity

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
